toolUseResult.prompt:
Design the implementation for a Chester voice-discipline upgrade. Optimize for **clean discipline composition** — the new C1 and C2 must integrate cleanly with existing partner-role disciplines (Translation Gate, Research Boundary, Private Precision Slot, Option-Naming Rule, Self-Evaluation) so the result reads as a coherent unified discipline, not five overlapping checklists. Accept more substantial edits to existing sections; accept more complex evaluation gate logic if it produces a cleaner whole.

## Brief Summary

Two new agent voice disciplines:

- **C1 (Externalized Coverage)** — agent must not reason from un-externalized context to a designer-facing conclusion. Load-bearing concepts must surface in designer-visible output before counting toward shared understanding. Single-session scope.
- **C2 (Fact default with marked departures)** — most claims unmarked (Facts; verifiable + repeatable). Assumptions explicitly marked with natural phrasing ("Assumption; I assumed...", "I'm assuming..."). Opinions explicitly marked with natural phrasing ("Opinion; I think...", "I recommend..."). HARD RULE: all recommendations are opinions. No source breadcrumb in commentary.

## Locked Constraints (non-negotiable)

- Translation Gate must remain functional (does its current job — strip code vocabulary from designer-visible output)
- Light-touch scope: only five files modified — partner role, start-bootstrap, MCP state.js + server.js, two design skills — plus one test file
- Cross-references in each design skill limited to ONE LINE each
- Citation system explicitly REJECTED
- Source breadcrumb in commentary explicitly REJECTED
- Existing partner-role sections must remain functional after edits — careful integrating edits OK; do not remove or break their core mechanism

## Three Other Lane 1 MVP Pieces

- Three telemetry fields added to MCP state: `groupSaturationHistory`, `transitionHistory` (per-round `{ ready, reasons }`), `warningsHistory` (score-jump warnings per round)
- Session metadata file at `design/{sprint-name}-session-meta.json` with sprint name, branch, timestamp, JSONL session ID, skill version
- Test confirming three telemetry fields persist across multiple submit cycles

## Your Axis

**Optimize for discipline composition.** The five existing disciplines (Translation Gate, Research Boundary, Private Precision Slot, Option-Naming Rule, Self-Evaluation) plus the two new ones (C1, C2) must read as a coherent unified discipline. Within the brief's locked constraints, your design should:

- Define an explicit RELATIONSHIP between C1, C2, and existing disciplines (do they layer? do they sequence? do they each address a different facet?)
- Possibly reorganize the partner-role file so the disciplines have a logical ordering (e.g., Research Boundary → C1 externalize → C2 mark → Translation Gate strip → Self-Evaluation check)
- Possibly extend the Self-Evaluation game to be a unified gate over all disciplines, not just "strategy talk vs code talk"
- Make explicit how the new disciplines compose with Private Precision Slot (where unmarked-Fact precision lives, what stays in private notes)

**Accept the sacrifice:** more edits to existing sections; the file structure may be more substantially reorganized. The integration cohesion is the win.

## F-A-C Self-Check

Before returning, verify your design passes:
- **Feasible** — fits Chester sprint constraints
- **Suitable** — solves voice discipline enforcement, telemetry capture, session metadata
- **Complete** — covers ALL six in-scope items including C1, C2, before/after example, three telemetry fields, session metadata file, telemetry test, plus cross-references

If your axis cannot satisfy all three, return null with reasoning.

## Codebase Context

Repository at `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/`. Key files:
- `skills/util-design-partner-role/SKILL.md`
- `skills/start-bootstrap/SKILL.md`
- `skills/design-large-task/understanding-mcp/state.js` and `server.js`
- `skills/design-large-task/SKILL.md`
- `skills/design-small-task/SKILL.md`
- Brief at `docs/chester/working/20260425-01-redesign-convergence-model/design/redesign-convergence-model-design-00.md`

## Output Structure

Return structured bulleted format (no tables):

- **Approach Summary** (2-3 sentences)
- **Axis Position** (where on composition axis; what is sacrificed)
- **Component Structure** (bullets of new or modified units)
- **Reuse Profile** (bullets of existing partner-role mechanisms leveraged)
- **Brief Compliance** (per-goal addressed-by, per-constraint respected-how, per-decision honored-where)
- **Risks Introduced** (bullets)
- **Feasibility Evidence** (cite specific constraints)
- **Suitability Evidence** (cite specific brief goals)
- **Completeness Evidence** (cite each in-scope item)

Do NOT include "Alternatives Considered."

content.text:
I now have everything I need. Let me synthesize the architecture design.

---

**Approach Summary**

The partner-role file is reorganized so the seven disciplines (Research Boundary, C1, C2, Private Precision Slot, Translation Gate, Option-Naming Rule, Self-Evaluation) follow a single pipeline logic: acquire context privately (Research Boundary), ensure that context surfaces before reasoning (C1), mark the epistemic status of every claim you make (C2), keep precision notes off the visible surface (Private Precision Slot), strip code vocabulary from what remains (Translation Gate), name options structurally (Option-Naming Rule), and gate the turn on all disciplines together (Self-Evaluation). The Self-Evaluation section is extended to cover all disciplines, not just "strategy talk vs code talk," producing a unified end-of-turn checklist. C1 and C2 are positioned mid-pipeline, not appended at the end, so they compose naturally with what comes before and after them.

**Axis Position**

Fully at the composition end of the axis. The sacrifice is that the existing partner-role file is substantially reorganized (section ordering changes, Self-Evaluation text is extended) — which the brief explicitly authorizes. What is preserved exactly: every existing section's core mechanism and its text. What changes: section order, section introduction prose that now explicitly names each discipline's place in the pipeline, and the Self-Evaluation checklist items. No existing discipline text is removed.

---

**Component Structure**

- `skills/util-design-partner-role/SKILL.md` — restructured as described below
- `skills/design-large-task/understanding-mcp/state.js` — additive changes to `initializeState` and `updateState`
- `skills/design-large-task/understanding-mcp/server.js` — additive change to `handleSubmitUnderstanding`
- `skills/start-bootstrap/SKILL.md` — new Step 4c writing session metadata JSON
- `skills/design-large-task/SKILL.md` — one-line cross-reference inserted in Understand Stage Per-Turn Flow Step 6 and in Solve Stage Per-Turn Flow Step 8
- `skills/design-small-task/SKILL.md` — one-line cross-reference inserted in Per-Turn Flow Step 3 ("Write commentary")
- `tests/test-telemetry-fields.sh` — new test file confirming three fields persist across three sequential submit cycles

---

**Reuse Profile**

- Research Boundary: retained verbatim; repositioned to slot 1 (private context acquisition). C1's "externalize before reasoning" requirement directly follows from it.
- Private Precision Slot: retained verbatim; repositioned to slot 4 (where precision goes after C1 surfaces concepts and C2 marks them). The placement makes explicit that uncensored precision lives in notes, while marked claims live in the conversation.
- Translation Gate: retained verbatim; repositioned to slot 5. Now clearly downstream of C2 — you mark first, then strip code vocabulary from the marked output. The two disciplines address orthogonal concerns: C2 marks epistemic status, Translation Gate strips vocabulary register.
- Option-Naming Rule: retained verbatim; slot 6. Still a point-in-time surface rule, not a pipeline rule.
- Self-Evaluation: text retained verbatim plus new sibling checks appended. This is the only discipline whose text grows materially. The existing positive-game framing is kept; the new checks read as natural siblings to "strategy talk vs code talk."
- Core Stance and Interpreter Frame: retained verbatim in their current position before the discipline pipeline begins.

---

**Partner-Role File Reorganization**

The file will use the following section ordering. Section headings remain identical to current where the section is preserved; only the two new sections and the Self-Evaluation extension are new text.

1. The Core Stance (unchanged, stays first — establishes the interpreter frame)
2. The Interpreter Frame (unchanged)
3. The Discipline Pipeline (new introductory paragraph — 2-3 sentences naming the pipeline structure)
4. **Research Boundary** (moved up from its current position after Commentary Model; becomes pipeline slot 1 — private context acquisition)
5. **C1: Externalized Coverage** (new; pipeline slot 2 — surfaces before reasoning)
6. **C2: Fact Default with Marked Departures** (new; pipeline slot 3 — marks epistemic status)
7. **Private Precision Slot** (repositioned from slot 3 in current file to slot 4; explicitly scoped as "where uncensored precision goes that C2 doesn't need to mark")
8. **Translation Gate** (repositioned to slot 5; downstream of marking — vocabulary stripping happens after epistemic status is set)
9. **Option-Naming Rule** (slot 6; point-in-time surface rule)
10. **Self-Evaluation** (slot 7; now a unified end-of-turn gate over all seven disciplines)
11. **Stance Principles** (unchanged, stays last)

The introductory paragraph for the pipeline section will read as: "Seven disciplines govern what you say to the designer and how. They form a pipeline, not a checklist — each addresses a different facet of a single discipline problem. Work through them in order before sending any turn."

**C1 Section Design**

Title: "C1: Externalized Coverage"

Content:
- Statement: The agent must not reason from un-externalized context to a designer-facing conclusion. If a concept is load-bearing in your reasoning — if removing it would change your conclusion — it must appear in designer-visible output before that conclusion lands.
- Single-session scope: this rule applies within the current session only. Carry-forward understanding from prior sessions does not require re-surfacing in the current session.
- Failure mode named: "silent premise" — the agent's commentary reaches a conclusion through reasoning the designer cannot see because the enabling context never appeared in designer-visible output. The designer cannot challenge what they cannot see.
- Interaction with Research Boundary: Research Boundary keeps code vocabulary private; C1 says that concepts extracted from that private research must surface in the information package before the commentary draws on them as shared premises.
- Interaction with Private Precision Slot: precision (type names, property shapes) stays private per the Private Precision Slot discipline. C1 does not require surfacing precision. It requires surfacing the concept — the design-level meaning extracted from that precision.
- A one-sentence example of the failure: "The commentary concluded 'this direction is risky' drawing on three patterns read during exploration, none of which appeared in the information package — the designer cannot evaluate the conclusion."

**C2 Section Design**

Title: "C2: Fact Default with Marked Departures"

Content:
- Default state: most claims are Facts — verifiable and repeatable. Leave them unmarked.
- Assumption marker: use when a claim rests on an unstated premise the designer hasn't confirmed. Natural phrasing is accepted: "Assumption; I assumed...", "I'm assuming...". Any phrasing that makes the assumption unambiguous is valid.
- Opinion marker: use when a claim represents your judgment, perspective, or preference — not what the system does but what you think about it. Natural phrasing: "Opinion; I think...", "I recommend...", "My read is...".
- Hard rule: all recommendations are opinions, regardless of how well-grounded the supporting Facts are. The act of recommending is the act of judging. A recommendation grounded in ten Facts is still your judgment applied to those Facts. It carries the Opinion marker.
- No source breadcrumb: do not add "(Fact; read at line 45)" or similar. Precision about sources lives in private notes via the Private Precision Slot. The designer requests source explicitly if needed.
- Interaction with Translation Gate: C2 markers do not introduce code vocabulary. The markers and their natural phrasing use only plain human language. Translation Gate runs after C2 marking and strips any code vocabulary from the marked output — the two disciplines are orthogonal.
- Before/after example (as required by acceptance criteria): design this to show at minimum one Fact, one Assumption with marker, one Opinion with marker, and one confidence-laundering case (a Fact-shaped sentence that should have been Assumption). Draw the example from a conceptual design domain, not from code (so it passes Translation Gate).

Example (to be included verbatim in the section):

Before (confidence laundering — Assumption presented as Fact):

> The diagnostic layer routes all warnings through a single aggregation point. The safest approach is to add the new signal there.

After (C2-compliant):

> The diagnostic layer routes all warnings through a single aggregation point. I'm assuming this aggregation point is the right injection target — it's the only one I found, but the codebase may carry injection conventions I didn't reach. I think the safest approach is to add the new signal there; the aggregation point already carries the structural plumbing, and widening it beats introducing a parallel path.

What changed: The first after-sentence adds the Assumption marker (the agent is relying on an un-confirmed premise: that no other injection target exists). The second after-sentence adds Opinion phrasing ("I think") because it is a recommendation. The first sentence (Fact — verifiable) stays unmarked.

**Self-Evaluation Section Design**

The existing section is retained verbatim. After the existing "Did this turn sound like strategy talk or code talk?" block, a new sibling block is appended:

"Also answer these before sending:

- Did I draw any conclusion from a premise the designer hasn't seen? If yes, surface the premise in the information package before the conclusion. (C1)
- Did I present an Assumption as a Fact? If yes, add the Assumption marker. (C2)
- Did I present an Opinion or recommendation without marking it? If yes, add the Opinion marker. (C2)
- Does this turn carry a recommendation without an Opinion marker? Recommendations are always opinions. (C2 hard rule)

These are siblings to the strategy-talk check, not replacements. If any answer is yes, fix it before sending."

The positive-game framing is preserved: all four checks are stated as conditions to correct, not prohibitions to avoid. The fix instruction ("fix it before sending") matches the existing game structure.

---

**State.js Changes**

In `initializeState` (line 22-28 of `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/skills/design-large-task/understanding-mcp/state.js`), add three new fields alongside the existing `scoreHistory` and `saturationHistory` empty arrays:

```
groupSaturationHistory: [],
transitionHistory: [],
warningsHistory: [],
```

In `updateState` (line 33-59), after the existing `next.saturationHistory.push(overall)` call, append:

```
next.groupSaturationHistory.push(structuredClone(groupSaturation));
next.transitionHistory.push({ ready: next.transition.ready, reasons: next.transition.reasons });
next.warningsHistory.push(/* see server.js design below */);
```

The challenge: `updateState` does not currently receive `warnings` — that comes from `validateUnderstandingSubmission` in server.js before `updateState` is called. Two options for threading warnings through:

Option A: Pass `warnings` as an additional argument to `updateState(state, scores, warnings)`. This is the cleaner approach — warnings are computed before updateState is called, so passing them in is one extra parameter. The server.js call becomes `updateState(state, scores, validation.warnings)`.

Option B: Push to `warningsHistory` in server.js after updateState returns, before saving.

Option A is cleaner. Use Option A. The `updateState` signature becomes `updateState(state, newScores, warnings = [])` and pushes `warnings` to `next.warningsHistory`.

The `transition` field is computed inside `updateState` via `checkTransitionReady(next)`, so `transitionHistory` can push from the result already computed in the function. The exact push is:

```js
next.transitionHistory.push({ ready: next.transition.ready, reasons: [...(next.transition.reasons ?? [])] });
```

**Server.js Changes**

In `handleSubmitUnderstanding` (lines 119-167), the call to `updateState` becomes `updateState(state, scores, validation.warnings)`.

The return value from `handleSubmitUnderstanding` does not need to expose the new telemetry arrays directly — the acceptance criteria says "new fields available via state inspection" (i.e., via `get_understanding_state`), not that submit must return them. The existing response shape is preserved. No changes to the returned JSON keys from `handleSubmitUnderstanding`.

The `handleGetState` function (lines 170-188) already returns `...state` spread, so `groupSaturationHistory`, `transitionHistory`, and `warningsHistory` will automatically appear in `get_understanding_state` responses once they're in the state object. No changes needed to `handleGetState`.

---

**Start-Bootstrap Changes**

After the existing Step 4b (Write Active Sprint Breadcrumb), add Step 4c:

**Step 4c: Write Session Metadata**

```bash
git -C "$(chester-config-read | grep CHESTER_PLANS_DIR | cut -d= -f2)/../.." rev-parse HEAD 2>/dev/null
```

Write `{CHESTER_WORKING_DIR}/{sprint-subdir}/design/{sprint-name}-session-meta.json` with:
- `sprintName`: the sprint subdirectory name (e.g., `20260425-01-redesign-convergence-model`)
- `branchName`: same as sprint subdirectory name (branch is created by `util-worktree` at closure, but the intended branch name is known now)
- `sessionStartTimestamp`: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp at the moment of write
- `jsonlSessionId`: read from `CLAUDE_SESSION_ID` env var if available; otherwise the JSONL file path `~/.claude/projects/{project-hash}/{session}.jsonl` — use the most recently modified `.jsonl` file in the active project directory
- `skillVersion`: object with two fields — `utilDesignPartnerRole` and `designLargeTask` — each containing the git commit hash of their respective SKILL.md files (`git log -1 --format=%H -- skills/util-design-partner-role/SKILL.md` and same for `skills/design-large-task/SKILL.md`), run with `git -C <repo-root>` to respect worktree cd hazard

The file is written with standard JSON indentation (2 spaces). The write step runs unconditionally — even if some fields cannot be resolved (e.g., `CLAUDE_SESSION_ID` is not set), write the file with null for that field rather than failing bootstrap.

Note in the SKILL.md text: "This file lives in `design/` alongside the brief artifacts; `finish-archive-artifacts` copies it to `plans/` at sprint close. It is tracked in git — do not gitignore."

---

**Design Skill Cross-Reference Changes**

In `design-large-task/SKILL.md`:
- Understand Stage Per-Turn Flow, Step 6 ("Write commentary") — append one line at the end of that step's description: "Before sending, verify C1 and C2 discipline from `util-design-partner-role` — every load-bearing premise is visible, every Assumption and Opinion is marked."
- Solve Stage Per-Turn Flow, Step 8 ("Write commentary") — append the same one line.

In `design-small-task/SKILL.md`:
- Per-Turn Flow Step 3 ("Write commentary") — append one line: "Before sending, verify C1 and C2 discipline from `util-design-partner-role` — every load-bearing premise is visible, every Assumption and Opinion is marked."

Each design skill gets exactly one cross-reference line per the constraint. The brief allows one line "in the per-turn flow" for each skill. The placement at the commentary step (not the Translation Gate step) is intentional: C1 and C2 govern what you say before vocabulary stripping, while Translation Gate governs vocabulary after. Placing the C1/C2 reminder at the commentary step and letting Translation Gate remain at the "Present to user" step preserves their distinct positions in the pipeline.

---

**Test File Design**

`/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/tests/test-telemetry-fields.sh`

Structure follows existing test patterns in the repo. The test:
1. Starts the understanding MCP server as a subprocess (or uses a direct JS invocation against state.js functions if the test harness allows unit-level testing)
2. Creates a temp state file
3. Calls `initialize_understanding` — verifies that `groupSaturationHistory`, `transitionHistory`, and `warningsHistory` exist as empty arrays in the state file
4. Calls `submit_understanding` three times with valid scores, incrementing at least one score slightly each time
5. Reads the state file after each call
6. Asserts: `groupSaturationHistory` has exactly 1, 2, 3 entries after calls 1, 2, 3
7. Asserts: `transitionHistory` has exactly 1, 2, 3 entries after calls 1, 2, 3
8. Asserts: `warningsHistory` has exactly 1, 2, 3 entries after calls 1, 2, 3
9. Asserts: each `transitionHistory` entry has `ready` and `reasons` fields
10. Asserts: `scoreHistory` and `saturationHistory` still have the same counts (existing fields not broken)
11. Cleans up temp files
12. Exits 0 on pass, 1 on fail with diagnostic output

The test uses `node` directly against the MCP server over stdin/stdout following the existing test pattern. Look at the existing test files to confirm the exact invocation pattern before writing — but since the MCP server communicates over stdio and existing tests likely use JSON-RPC message framing, the test will send raw JSON-RPC requests and parse responses.

---

**Implementation Map**

Files to modify:

- `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/skills/util-design-partner-role/SKILL.md` — substantial reorganization: add pipeline introduction paragraph, reorder Research Boundary to slot 1, add C1 and C2 as new sections in slots 2-3, reposition Private Precision Slot to slot 4, reposition Translation Gate to slot 5, keep Option-Naming Rule at slot 6, extend Self-Evaluation with four sibling checks, keep Stance Principles last. All existing section text preserved verbatim; only section order and new content changes.

- `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/skills/design-large-task/understanding-mcp/state.js` — add three fields to `initializeState` return object; update `updateState` signature to accept `warnings = []`; add three push operations in `updateState`.

- `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/skills/design-large-task/understanding-mcp/server.js` — update `updateState` call in `handleSubmitUnderstanding` to pass `validation.warnings` as third argument.

- `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/skills/start-bootstrap/SKILL.md` — add Step 4c after Step 4b.

- `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/skills/design-large-task/SKILL.md` — add one-line cross-reference at end of Understand Stage Step 6 and Solve Stage Step 8.

- `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/skills/design-small-task/SKILL.md` — add one-line cross-reference at end of Per-Turn Flow Step 3.

Files to create:

- `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/tests/test-telemetry-fields.sh` — new test file as described above.

---

**Data Flow**

C1/C2 in a live session turn:

1. Agent reads code privately → Research Boundary
2. Agent extracts concept from code → concept is now held internally
3. C1 check: is this concept load-bearing for the commentary I'm about to write? If yes, it must appear in the information package before the commentary. Agent places it in "Current facts" or "Surface analysis."
4. C2 check: as agent writes each sentence of information package and commentary — is this a Fact (verifiable, repeatable)? Leave unmarked. Is this resting on an unconfirmed premise? Add Assumption phrasing. Is this judgment, preference, or recommendation? Add Opinion phrasing.
5. Private Precision Slot: precision (type names) captured via `capture_thought` with tag `private-precision`. Does not appear in output.
6. Translation Gate: strip all code vocabulary from the marked, externalized output.
7. Self-Evaluation: answer five questions (strategy/code + four C1/C2 siblings) before sending. Fix any yes answers.
8. Send.

Telemetry flow per submit cycle:

1. `handleSubmitUnderstanding` calls `validateUnderstandingSubmission` → receives `validation.warnings`
2. Calls `updateState(state, scores, validation.warnings)` — new signature
3. `updateState` computes `groupSaturation`, `overall`, `transition`
4. Pushes to `groupSaturationHistory`, `transitionHistory` (from computed transition), `warningsHistory` (from passed-in warnings)
5. Saves state file
6. `handleGetState` later spreads full state — all three new arrays visible

---

**Build Sequence**

Phase 1 — MCP state changes (no behavioral risk, purely additive):
- [ ] Add three fields to `initializeState` in `state.js`
- [ ] Update `updateState` signature and add three push operations in `state.js`
- [ ] Update `updateState` call in `handleSubmitUnderstanding` in `server.js`
- [ ] Write `tests/test-telemetry-fields.sh` and verify it passes

Phase 2 — Bootstrap change (additive, low risk):
- [ ] Add Step 4c to `start-bootstrap/SKILL.md`
- [ ] Verify step text is consistent with existing step 4b format

Phase 3 — Partner role file reorganization (highest-stakes edit; do this in one commit):
- [ ] Write out new section order with pipeline introduction
- [ ] Insert C1 section in slot 2
- [ ] Insert C2 section (with before/after example) in slot 3
- [ ] Reposition Research Boundary to slot 1 (text preserved verbatim)
- [ ] Reposition Private Precision Slot to slot 4 (text preserved verbatim)
- [ ] Reposition Translation Gate to slot 5 (text preserved verbatim)
- [ ] Keep Option-Naming Rule at slot 6 (text preserved verbatim)
- [ ] Extend Self-Evaluation with four sibling checks
- [ ] Read the full file aloud mentally to confirm it reads as a coherent pipeline narrative

Phase 4 — Design skill cross-references (minimal edits):
- [ ] Add one-line reference at Understand Stage Step 6 in `design-large-task/SKILL.md`
- [ ] Add one-line reference at Solve Stage Step 8 in `design-large-task/SKILL.md`
- [ ] Add one-line reference at Per-Turn Flow Step 3 in `design-small-task/SKILL.md`

Phase 5 — Verification:
- [ ] Confirm existing mechanics test (if any) still passes
- [ ] Confirm `test-telemetry-fields.sh` passes
- [ ] Confirm partner-role file contains no code vocabulary in new C1/C2 sections or extended Self-Evaluation
- [ ] Confirm `start-bootstrap` step count sequence is intact (4a, 4b, 4c)

---

**Critical Details**

Error handling in `state.js`: the `updateState` warnings parameter must default to `[]` to ensure backward compatibility if any caller does not pass it. The push to `warningsHistory` is always executed (even if warnings is empty array) so the array length stays synchronized with round number — each round adds exactly one entry to each of the three new arrays.

The `transition` object from `checkTransitionReady` may have `reasons` as either an array or undefined depending on implementation. The push should normalize: `reasons: next.transition.reasons ?? []` to ensure `warningsHistory` entries are always objects with consistent shape.

The before/after example in C2 must not use any proper noun from the Chester codebase (no "MCP", no "capture_thought", no file names) — it must be a generic design domain example that passes Translation Gate when read aloud. The diagnostic layer example above is clean.

The C1 section must make clear that the single-session scope is tight: it does not require the agent to re-surface prior-sprint conclusions within the current session. It governs within-session private-to-public reasoning chains only. This prevents C1 from becoming an obligation to summarize all prior context every turn.

The skill version capture in session metadata uses `git -C <repo-root>` to respect the worktree cd hazard established in `feedback_worktree_cd_hazard.md`. The repo root is resolvable from `CHESTER_PLANS_DIR` (which is a relative path; the root is its grandparent) or from the `git -C "$(pwd)" rev-parse --show-toplevel` pattern.

---

**Brief Compliance**

Per-goal addressed-by:
- C1 (Externalized Coverage): C1 section in partner-role, positioned as pipeline slot 2 after Research Boundary
- C2 (Fact default with marked departures): C2 section with before/after example in partner-role slot 3; hard rule for recommendations included
- Before/after example demonstrating C2: included in C2 section, satisfies four required elements (Fact, Assumption, Opinion, confidence-laundering case)
- Three telemetry fields: `groupSaturationHistory`, `transitionHistory`, `warningsHistory` in `state.js` and `server.js`
- Session metadata file: Step 4c in `start-bootstrap/SKILL.md`
- Test confirming telemetry persistence: `tests/test-telemetry-fields.sh`

Per-constraint respected-how:
- Translation Gate functional: retained verbatim, repositioned but not modified; C2 markers use plain language only; Translation Gate explicitly positioned downstream of C2 in the pipeline
- Light-touch scope: exactly five skill files plus one test file; no gate logic, no downstream skills touched
- Cross-references limited to one line each: one line inserted in Understand Stage commentary step, one in Solve Stage commentary step for large-task; one line in Per-Turn Flow Step 3 for small-task
- Citation system rejected: C2 discipline uses natural phrasing markers only; no numbered inline markers, no sources document
- Source breadcrumb rejected: C2 section explicitly names the no-breadcrumb rule; precision lives in Private Precision Slot
- Existing sections intact: all five existing discipline sections (Research Boundary, Private Precision Slot, Translation Gate, Option-Naming Rule, Self-Evaluation) have their core text preserved verbatim; only positioning and the Self-Evaluation extension change

Per-decision honored-where:
- Self-Evaluation gains sibling check (not replacement): existing positive-game framing preserved; four new checks appended as siblings; fix instruction mirrors existing game structure
- C2 natural phrasing: section specifies "natural phrasing accepted" and gives multiple acceptable forms for both markers
- All recommendations are opinions: stated as hard rule in C2 section
- Discipline in partner-role, inherited by both skills: exactly this structure implemented; design skills get one-line reference only

---

**Risks Introduced**

- The partner-role file reorganization is a substantial edit to a file both design skills depend on. If section ordering is done carelessly, a section's prose may reference a section that now comes later in the file, creating internal forward references that confuse the agent reading it. Mitigation: the pipeline introduction paragraph names all seven disciplines in order, so even if individual sections reference each other, the overall ordering is declared up front.

- C1 "load-bearing concept" is a judgment call. The agent must decide what counts as load-bearing. If the definition is too loose, C1 becomes a mandate to externalize everything (collapsing into information dump). If too tight, the agent games it by classifying enabling premises as "background knowledge." The section must include the test: "would removing this from the designer-visible output change whether the designer could challenge my conclusion?" That is the operational test.

- C2 "Assumption" marker is sticky. Once the agent starts marking assumptions, it may over-mark — applying the marker to facts that are merely uncertain rather than genuinely resting on unconfirmed premises. The distinction: an Assumption is something the designer could confirm or deny; an uncertain Fact is something that is just hard to know. Over-marking dilutes the signal. The section should include this distinction.

- The `updateState` signature change from two args to three is backward-compatible via the default `warnings = []`, but any future caller that passes scores as a positional third argument would silently fail in a surprising way. Low probability given current codebase but worth noting.

- The session metadata `jsonlSessionId` field is best-effort. `CLAUDE_SESSION_ID` may not be set in all invocation contexts. The most-recently-modified JSONL fallback is fragile in multi-session environments. The metadata write must not fail bootstrap; a null or "unknown" value for this one field is acceptable.

---

**Feasibility Evidence**

- Five files plus one test is the stated constraint and this design touches exactly those files. The reorganization of partner-role is a text edit, not a code change. The state.js and server.js changes are three field additions and one argument thread. The start-bootstrap change is one new step. All fit comfortably within a single Chester sprint.
- The test file pattern follows existing `tests/` bash scripts and requires only that the MCP server accept JSON-RPC over stdio, which it already does (server.js line 193-197 shows StdioServerTransport).
- No new dependencies are introduced. No schema changes outside the three named fields. No new MCP tools.

---

**Suitability Evidence**

- C1 directly addresses "confidence laundering" (agent reasoning from un-externalized premises) identified in the brief's Goal section as the primary failure mode.
- C2 directly addresses "agent presents pattern-matched guesses with the same fluency as verified observations" — the Assumption/Opinion/Fact distinction makes epistemic status visible at claim level.
- The pipeline reorganization makes the composition relationship explicit rather than leaving five disciplines as an unordered list. The brief's axis goal is "read as a coherent unified discipline, not five overlapping checklists" — the pipeline structure with explicit slot ordering achieves this.
- The unified Self-Evaluation gate over all seven disciplines gives the agent one question per discipline to answer before sending, which is operationally cleaner than checking disciplines at scattered points in the per-turn flow.

---

**Completeness Evidence**

- C1 (Externalized Coverage): new section in partner-role, pipeline slot 2, with statement, failure mode named, scope clarified, interaction with Research Boundary and Private Precision Slot explicit
- C2 (Fact default with marked departures): new section in partner-role, pipeline slot 3, with default-state rule, Assumption marker with natural phrasing, Opinion marker with natural phrasing, hard rule for recommendations, no-breadcrumb constraint, interaction with Translation Gate explicit
- Before/after example demonstrating C2: included in C2 section with at least one Fact, one Assumption, one Opinion, one confidence-laundering case
- Self-Evaluation sibling check: four new checks appended to Self-Evaluation section, covering C1 and C2 obligations
- Three telemetry fields: `groupSaturationHistory`, `transitionHistory`, `warningsHistory` in `initializeState` and `updateState` in `state.js`; `updateState` call updated in `server.js`
- Session metadata file: Step 4c in `start-bootstrap/SKILL.md` with all five required fields (`sprintName`, `branchName`, `sessionStartTimestamp`, `jsonlSessionId`, `skillVersion`)
- Test file: `tests/test-telemetry-fields.sh` verifying all three fields persist across three sequential submit cycles, with assertion on both new and existing fields
- Cross-references: one line in Understand Stage Step 6 in `design-large-task/SKILL.md`, one line in Solve Stage Step 8 in `design-large-task/SKILL.md`, one line in Per-Turn Flow Step 3 in `design-small-task/SKILL.md`

All six in-scope items are covered. F-A-C: Feasible (five files, no new dependencies, test follows existing pattern), Suitable (addresses both voice discipline failure modes), Complete (all six items accounted for including before/after example and all five required metadata fields).

# Architect Comparison + Hybrid

## Framing

Two sharpest tensions identified in the brief:
- **Axis 1 (Architect A):** enforcement reach within light-touch — how reliably agent applies C1+C2 every turn
- **Axis 2 (Architect B):** discipline composition — how C1+C2 integrate with existing partner-role rules into a coherent whole

Hybrid type: **principled merge** — combines A's foregrounding intent and dual-placement cross-refs with B's pipeline introduction concept and four-sibling Self-Evaluation extension, without B's full reorganization.

## Prior Art Context

Eight prior sprints inform the design:

- **20260423-01 (refactor-chester-skills):** established the single-location-with-cross-refs pattern this sprint extends
- **20260408-03 (plan-mode-design-guard):** built the proof MCP that demonstrates a full citation system is feasible — but explicitly rejected by this sprint as MVP overhead
- **20260408-01 (compaction-hooks):** proved MCP state files survive session boundaries — three new telemetry fields inherit this
- **20260404-01 (architect-interview-review):** found agent's mental model stays code-anchored even when vocabulary stripped — this is exactly the failure mode C1 addresses
- **20260424-02 (masterplan-mode):** classified discipline mechanisms as prohibitions-via-tooling vs positive-games-via-voice-rules; C1/C2 are positive-games (siblings to Self-Evaluation)
- **2026-03-27 (socratic-interview):** proved two-layer pattern (persona + vocabulary enforcement) beats prohibitions
- **20260424-01 (build-decision-loop):** decision-loop provenance infrastructure — current session metadata complements but doesn't depend on it
- **20260405-01 (architect-round-one):** redirecting completion drive toward concrete artifacts (scoring) succeeded — telemetry additions extend this

**Bearing on each block:** All three approaches honor the established pattern (single-location discipline + cross-refs). None reintroduce the rejected citation system. Agent A and Hybrid foreground C1/C2 visually; Architect B's full reorganization is more invasive than the existing pattern requires.

---

## Block A — Architect A (Enforcement Reach)

- **Approach:** C1 and C2 inserted BEFORE Interpreter Frame in partner-role (early in file read order, foregrounded). Self-Evaluation extended with one sibling check. Dual cross-reference in large-task (Understand Step 7 + Solve Step 9, both at Translation Gate checklist step). One cross-ref in small-task.
- **Where on axis:** high-enforcement end. Sacrifices surface size in partner-role with insertions ahead of existing sections.
- **Telemetry:** standard append in `state.js`; `updateState` signature changes to accept `warnings`; `server.js` passes `validation.warnings`.
- **Session metadata:** new Step 6 in start-bootstrap, after Step 4b breadcrumb.
- **Test:** direct Node import of state.js functions (lighter than full MCP transport test).
- **Risk:** "load-bearing concept" is unquantified — agent can comply superficially. Foregrounding shifts read order, but Translation Gate moves slightly later.

## Block B — Architect B (Discipline Composition)

- **Approach:** Full partner-role reorganization into a 7-discipline pipeline (Research Boundary → C1 → C2 → Private Precision Slot → Translation Gate → Option-Naming → Self-Evaluation). New "The Discipline Pipeline" introductory paragraph. Self-Evaluation extended with FOUR sibling checks covering both C1 and C2 obligations. Cross-references at commentary step (Understand Step 6, Solve Step 8, small-task Step 3).
- **Where on axis:** full composition end. Sacrifices: substantial reorganization of file ordering; existing section text preserved verbatim but positioning changes.
- **Telemetry:** same as A (consensus on `updateState` signature change).
- **Session metadata:** Step 4c in start-bootstrap (adjacent to existing breadcrumb step 4b).
- **Test:** bash + Node JSON-RPC over stdio.
- **Risk:** reorganization is invasive; risk of breaking subtle internal references; "load-bearing concept" same operational ambiguity as A; `jsonlSessionId` capture is best-effort.

## Block C — Hybrid Recommendation (Principled Merge)

- **Approach:** Add a SHORT 2-sentence introductory paragraph to partner-role naming C1, C2 as new disciplines composing with existing rules — without forcing full pipeline ordering. Insert C1 and C2 sections after Private Precision Slot, before Option-Naming Rule (preserves existing section order; positions C1/C2 logically within the voice-discipline cluster). C2 includes before/after example. Self-Evaluation extended with B's FOUR sibling checks (more thorough than A's one). Cross-references at commentary step in EACH per-turn flow: large-task Understand Step 6 + Solve Step 8 + small-task Phase 4 Step 3.
- **Where on axis:** between A and B. Optimization target: highest enforcement signal that does NOT require file reorganization; uses ordering already established by prior sprint pattern. Sacrifices: less aggressive foregrounding than A (C1/C2 not literally first); less explicit pipeline narrative than B.
- **Telemetry:** consensus from A and B — three fields in `initializeState`, three pushes in `updateState`, signature change to accept `warnings` parameter, `server.js` passes `validation.warnings`.
- **Session metadata:** Step 4c in start-bootstrap (B's placement; cleaner than A's Step 6 — adjacent to breadcrumb step).
- **Test:** A's direct Node import approach (simpler than JSON-RPC framing).
- **F-A-C:** Feasible (5 files + 1 test, no infra dependencies). Suitable (addresses both voice discipline failure modes from brief). Complete (all 6 in-scope items + cross-refs + before/after example).
- **Risk:** "load-bearing concept" remains unquantified across all three options — operational test ("would removing this change whether designer can challenge my conclusion?") added to C1 section text. Cross-ref count: brief says "one-line cross-reference" singular per skill; both architects interpret as one per per-turn-flow (so 2 in large-task because it has two stages). Open to designer interpretation.

---

## Recommendation Reasoning

Hybrid optimizes for enforcement signal without invasive file reorganization. Reasoning:

- A's foregrounding (insertions ahead of Interpreter Frame) creates internal ordering tension; reader sees C1/C2 before the file's voice-establishment text
- B's full pipeline reorganization is more change than the brief's "light-touch" spirit comfortably allows; existing section ordering has worked across prior sprints; reorganizing risks subtle breakage
- Hybrid keeps existing ordering, adds a short introductory hook explaining composition, places C1/C2 logically within the voice cluster, takes B's four-sibling Self-Evaluation extension (more thorough discipline coverage), takes A's lighter test approach
- Telemetry consensus across both architects is reliable signal — both independently arrived at the same shape, so adopt it
- Session metadata at Step 4c (B's placement) is cleaner than A's Step 6 — sits next to existing breadcrumb write

## Choose Direction

(a) Architect A — Foreground C1/C2 ahead of Interpreter Frame; one Self-Evaluation sibling check; dual cross-ref in large-task at Translation Gate step
(b) Architect B — Full 7-discipline pipeline reorganization; four Self-Evaluation sibling checks; cross-refs at commentary step
(c) Hybrid Recommendation — Add C1/C2 between Private Precision Slot and Option-Naming Rule; short composition intro; four sibling checks; cross-refs at commentary step
(d) Articulate your own variant — name what

content: Building Lane 1 voice discipline (C1 + C2) for Chester partner role with telemetry additions; currently in design-specify, three architect options presented for the implementation approach. Next: pick option (a), (b), (c), or (d) to proceed.

1	# Spec Template (Loop-Optimized)
2	
3	Canonical spec document format used by `design-specify`. This template is loop-optimized: each acceptance criterion carries an observable boundary, a stable ID, and a skeleton-stub reference so that `execute-write` can couple code to tests through the spec.
4	
5	## When to Use
6	
7	Copy this template when producing the spec document at `spec/{sprint-name}-spec-NN.md`. Fill in every section. The Acceptance Criteria block is the primary hand-off surface: plan-build turns criteria into tasks, and execute-write's decision loop resolves every propagation event back to an AC clause.
8	
9	Leave `Implementing tasks:` and `Decisions:` empty at spec-write time — those fields are populated downstream (plan-build and execute-write, respectively).
10	
11	## Template
12	
13	```markdown
14	# Spec: {Feature Name}
15	
16	**Sprint:** YYYYMMDD-##-verb-noun-noun
17	**Parent brief:** {design brief path}
18	**Architecture:** {architecture chosen from design-specify hybrid}
19	
20	## Goal
21	{one paragraph}
22	
23	## Components
24	{new/modified units}
25	
26	## Data Flow
27	{how data moves through components}
28	
29	## Error Handling
30	{failure modes and responses}
31	
32	## Testing Strategy
33	{test categories, coverage expectations}
34	
35	## Constraints
36	{cross-cutting constraints}
37	
38	## Non-Goals
39	{explicitly out of scope}
40	
41	## Acceptance Criteria
42	
43	### AC-{N.M} — {Short Name}
44	
45	**Observable boundary:**
46	- {condition → outcome}
47	- {condition → outcome}
48	
49	**Given:** {precondition}
50	**When:** {trigger}
51	**Then:** {observable result}
52	
53	**Test skeleton ID:** `ac-{N-M}-{slug}` (auto-scaffolded at design-specify time; skeleton stub at language-appropriate path; skeleton manifest at `spec/{sprint-name}-spec-skeleton-00.md`)
54	
55	**Implementing tasks:** (populated by plan-build)
56	
57	**Decisions:** (populated by execute-write)
58	```
59	
60	## Notes
61	
62	- **Stable IDs:** `AC-{N.M}` IDs are immutable once the spec is approved. When `execute-write` refines an existing clause, it uses a suffix form (`AC-N.Ma`) rather than renumbering.
63	- **Observable boundary** is mandatory. Every criterion must declare what a test can observe from outside the unit. If a criterion cannot be stated as an observable boundary, split or reformulate it.
64	- **Skeleton IDs** match the stub emitted by `skeleton-generator.md`. The manifest file indexes every skeleton by ID so `execute-write` can look up the stub during propagation.
65	- Repeat the AC block for each criterion. Numbering is per-section (`AC-1.1`, `AC-1.2`, `AC-2.1`, ...).
66

1	# Skeleton Generator
2	
3	Language-aware procedure that scaffolds a failing test stub for every acceptance criterion in the spec. Run at `design-specify` time, after the spec's Acceptance Criteria section is frozen. Output lands in the project's test tree; an index manifest is written to `spec/{sprint-name}-spec-skeleton-00.md` (per `util-artifact-schema`'s `spec-skeleton` artifact type).
4	
5	## 1. Detection Procedure
6	
7	Walk the project root and pick the first match. Stop at the first hit — do not merge toolchains.
8	
9	| Priority | Signal                                | Language   | Test directory (default)   |
10	|----------|---------------------------------------|------------|----------------------------|
11	| 1        | `Cargo.toml` at project root          | Rust       | `tests/` or `src/*_test.rs`|
12	| 2        | `package.json` at project root        | TypeScript | `test/` or `__tests__/`    |
13	| 3        | `pyproject.toml` at project root      | Python     | `tests/`                   |
14	| 4        | first `.sh` file under `tests/`       | Bash       | `tests/`                   |
15	| 5        | none of the above                     | Unknown    | (markdown fallback)        |
16	
17	If more than one signal fires, prefer the higher-priority one and note the choice in the manifest.
18	
19	**Unknown fallback:** emit a plain markdown skeleton stub (`spec/skeletons/ac-{N-M}-{slug}.md`) and flag the fallback to the user — the designer must confirm the test framework before execute-write runs.
20	
21	## 2. Per-Language Stub Generation
22	
23	Each skeleton is a single failing test named for the criterion. The test name (function, `it(...)` label, or function identifier) is exactly the skeleton ID: `ac-{N-M}-{slug}`. Slugs are kebab-case derived from the AC short name.
24	
25	### Rust
26	
27	```rust
28	#[test]
29	fn ac_1_1_empty_input_rejected() {
30	    todo!("AC-1.1 — empty input rejected");
31	}
32	```
33	
34	Landing path: `tests/ac_1_1_empty_input_rejected.rs` (one file per criterion keeps diff churn minimal) or append to an existing `tests/acceptance.rs` if the crate prefers a single integration target.
35	
36	### TypeScript
37	
38	```ts
39	it('ac-1-1-empty-input-rejected', () => {
40	  throw new Error('pending: AC-1.1 — empty input rejected');
41	});
42	```
43	
44	Landing path: `test/acceptance/ac-1-1-empty-input-rejected.test.ts`.
45	
46	### Python
47	
48	```python
49	import pytest
50	
51	def test_ac_1_1_empty_input_rejected():
52	    pytest.skip("pending: AC-1.1 — empty input rejected")
53	```
54	
55	Landing path: `tests/test_ac_1_1_empty_input_rejected.py`.
56	
57	### Bash
58	
59	```bash
60	test_ac_1_1_empty_input_rejected() {
61	  echo "pending: AC-1.1 — empty input rejected"
62	  return 1
63	}
64	```
65	
66	Landing path: append to `tests/test-acceptance.sh` or write `tests/test-ac-1-1-empty-input-rejected.sh`. Bash stubs intentionally `return 1` so the harness sees a failing test until execute-write fills them in.
67	
68	### Unknown (markdown fallback)
69	
70	```markdown
71	# Skeleton: ac-1-1-empty-input-rejected
72	
73	AC-1.1 — empty input rejected. No language detected; re-run after test framework is chosen.
74	```
75	
76	## 3. Where Stubs Land
77	
78	- Stub files live in the project's normal test directory so the existing test runner discovers them without configuration.
79	- One stub per criterion keeps propagation diffs surgical (execute-write edits exactly one file per FIRE event).
80	- If an AC block is split or refined later, the new suffix ID (`AC-1.1a`) gets its own stub — the old stub is never renamed.
81	
82	## 4. Skeleton Manifest Format
83	
84	The manifest is the index execute-write consults during propagation. Write it to `spec/{sprint-name}-spec-skeleton-00.md` (the `spec-skeleton` artifact type in `util-artifact-schema`).
85	
86	```markdown
87	# Skeleton Manifest — {sprint-name}
88	
89	**Spec:** `spec/{sprint-name}-spec-NN.md`
90	**Language detected:** {rust|typescript|python|bash|unknown}
91	**Detection signal:** {Cargo.toml | package.json | pyproject.toml | tests/*.sh | none}
92	
93	## Skeletons
94	
95	| Skeleton ID                    | Criterion | Stub path                                   | Status  |
96	|--------------------------------|-----------|---------------------------------------------|---------|
97	| `ac-1-1-empty-input-rejected`  | AC-1.1    | `tests/ac_1_1_empty_input_rejected.rs`      | pending |
98	| `ac-1-2-whitespace-trimmed`    | AC-1.2    | `tests/ac_1_2_whitespace_trimmed.rs`        | pending |
99	```
100	
101	`Status` is `pending` at scaffold time and flips to `filled` once execute-write's `test-generator` subagent writes the real test body. The propagation procedure reads this manifest to know which skeletons still need filling.
102

# Skeleton Generator

Language-aware procedure that scaffolds a failing test stub for every acceptance criterion in the spec. Run at `design-specify` time, after the spec's Acceptance Criteria section is frozen. Output lands in the project's test tree; an index manifest is written to `spec/{sprint-name}-spec-skeleton-00.md` (per `util-artifact-schema`'s `spec-skeleton` artifact type).

## 1. Detection Procedure

Walk the project root and pick the first match. Stop at the first hit — do not merge toolchains.

| Priority | Signal                                | Language   | Test directory (default)   |
|----------|---------------------------------------|------------|----------------------------|
| 1        | `Cargo.toml` at project root          | Rust       | `tests/` or `src/*_test.rs`|
| 2        | `package.json` at project root        | TypeScript | `test/` or `__tests__/`    |
| 3        | `pyproject.toml` at project root      | Python     | `tests/`                   |
| 4        | first `.sh` file under `tests/`       | Bash       | `tests/`                   |
| 5        | none of the above                     | Unknown    | (markdown fallback)        |

If more than one signal fires, prefer the higher-priority one and note the choice in the manifest.

**Unknown fallback:** emit a plain markdown skeleton stub (`spec/skeletons/ac-{N-M}-{slug}.md`) and flag the fallback to the user — the designer must confirm the test framework before execute-write runs.

## 2. Per-Language Stub Generation

Each skeleton is a single failing test named for the criterion. The test name (function, `it(...)` label, or function identifier) is exactly the skeleton ID: `ac-{N-M}-{slug}`. Slugs are kebab-case derived from the AC short name.

### Rust

```rust
#[test]
fn ac_1_1_empty_input_rejected() {
    todo!("AC-1.1 — empty input rejected");
}
```

Landing path: `tests/ac_1_1_empty_input_rejected.rs` (one file per criterion keeps diff churn minimal) or append to an existing `tests/acceptance.rs` if the crate prefers a single integration target.

### TypeScript

```ts
it('ac-1-1-empty-input-rejected', () => {
  throw new Error('pending: AC-1.1 — empty input rejected');
});
```

Landing path: `test/acceptance/ac-1-1-empty-input-rejected.test.ts`.

### Python

```python
import pytest

def test_ac_1_1_empty_input_rejected():
    pytest.skip("pending: AC-1.1 — empty input rejected")
```

Landing path: `tests/test_ac_1_1_empty_input_rejected.py`.

### Bash

```bash
test_ac_1_1_empty_input_rejected() {
  echo "pending: AC-1.1 — empty input rejected"
  return 1
}
```

Landing path: append to `tests/test-acceptance.sh` or write `tests/test-ac-1-1-empty-input-rejected.sh`. Bash stubs intentionally `return 1` so the harness sees a failing test until execute-write fills them in.

### Unknown (markdown fallback)

```markdown
# Skeleton: ac-1-1-empty-input-rejected

AC-1.1 — empty input rejected. No language detected; re-run after test framework is chosen.
```

## 3. Where Stubs Land

- Stub files live in the project's normal test directory so the existing test runner discovers them without configuration.
- One stub per criterion keeps propagation diffs surgical (execute-write edits exactly one file per FIRE event).
- If an AC block is split or refined later, the new suffix ID (`AC-1.1a`) gets its own stub — the old stub is never renamed.

## 4. Skeleton Manifest Format

The manifest is the index execute-write consults during propagation. Write it to `spec/{sprint-name}-spec-skeleton-00.md` (the `spec-skeleton` artifact type in `util-artifact-schema`).

```markdown
# Skeleton Manifest — {sprint-name}

**Spec:** `spec/{sprint-name}-spec-NN.md`
**Language detected:** {rust|typescript|python|bash|unknown}
**Detection signal:** {Cargo.toml | package.json | pyproject.toml | tests/*.sh | none}

## Skeletons

| Skeleton ID                    | Criterion | Stub path                                   | Status  |
|--------------------------------|-----------|---------------------------------------------|---------|
| `ac-1-1-empty-input-rejected`  | AC-1.1    | `tests/ac_1_1_empty_input_rejected.rs`      | pending |
| `ac-1-2-whitespace-trimmed`    | AC-1.2    | `tests/ac_1_2_whitespace_trimmed.rs`        | pending |
```

`Status` is `pending` at scaffold time and flips to `filled` once execute-write's `test-generator` subagent writes the real test body. The propagation procedure reads this manifest to know which skeletons still need filling.

57	| `##` | Next available sequence number for that date | `01` |
58	| `verb-noun-noun` | Three lowercase hyphenated words: a verb (the action) followed by two nouns (the target). Derived from the problem statement. | `strip-console-reports` |
59

67	- **Branch name:** `20260407-01-strip-console-reports`
68	- **File prefix:** `strip-console-reports-{artifact}-{nn}.md`

78	├── design/     ← design briefs (proof envelope), thinking summaries, process evidence
79	├── spec/       ← specification documents, spec-stage ground-truth reports (opt-in)
80	├── plan/       ← implementation plans, threat reports, deferred items

89	         "{CHESTER_WORKING_DIR}/{sprint-subdir}/summary"
90	```
91	
92	## File Naming
93

95	
96	| Part | Rule |
97	|------|------|
98	| `{sprint-name}` | The three-word verb-noun-noun portion only (e.g., `strip-console-reports`) |
99	| `{artifact}` | The artifact type (see table below) |
100	| `{nn}` | Zero-padded version number: `00` is the original, `01`, `02`, `03` for revisions |

102	### Artifact Types
103	
104	| Artifact | Directory | Purpose | Produced by |
105	|----------|-----------|---------|-------------|
106	| `design` | `design/` | Design brief — proof envelope (goal, necessary conditions, rules, permissions, evidence, industry context, risks, acceptance criteria). Templates live in each design skill: `design-large-task/references/design-brief-template.md` (8-section envelope) and `design-small-task/references/design-brief-small-template.md` (6-section lightweight). | `design-large-task` (8-section envelope), `design-small-task` (6-section lightweight) |
107	| `thinking` | `design/` | Thinking summary — decision history of how the proof reached its necessary conditions | `design-large-task` |
108	| `process` | `design/` | Process evidence — operational narrative (Understand Stage saturation history, Stage Transition timing, Solve Stage length) | `design-large-task` |
109	| `spec` | `spec/` | Specification document — architecture chosen from the brief's envelope, component structure, reuse profile, trade-off profile | `design-specify` |
110	| `spec-ground-truth-report` | `spec/` | Ground-truth findings — codebase verification of spec claims about existing code (opt-in review) | `design-specify` (ground-truth review stage) |
111	| `spec-skeleton` | `spec/` | Skeleton manifest — indexes test stubs by acceptance criterion ID (`AC-N.M`) for execute-write's structural trigger-check coverage diff. Filename: `{sprint-name}-spec-skeleton-{nn}.md`. | `design-specify` (scaffolding step); consumed by `execute-write` (trigger-check step) |
112	| `plan` | `plan/` | Implementation plan — task-by-task build instructions | `plan-build` |
113	| `plan-threat-report` | `plan/` | Combined plan-attack + plan-smell findings | `plan-build` (hardening phase) |
114	| `deferred` | `plan/` | Items deferred during execution | `execute-write` |
115	| `summary` | `summary/` | Session summary — what happened and why | `finish-write-records` |
116	| `audit` | `summary/` | Reasoning audit — decision-level trace | `finish-write-records` |
117	
118	### State Files (Non-Artifact)

124	| `{sprint-name}-understanding-state.json` | `design/` | Understanding MCP state (Understand Stage nine-dimension saturation scoring) |
125	| `{sprint-name}-proof-state.json` | `design/` | Design Proof MCP state (Solve Stage necessary-conditions proof) |

129	- `00` is always the original
130	- When a skill revises an artifact (e.g., spec reviewer finds issues), it writes
131	  the next version: `01`, `02`, etc.
132	- The latest version is the current one — skills should read the highest-numbered
133	  version when loading an artifact

135	
136	## Refactor Artifacts

139

142	├── {slug}-brief-00.md      ← evaluation brief (unique to refactors)
143	├── {slug}-summary-00.md    ← session summary (same format as sprint summaries)
144	└── {slug}-audit-00.md      ← reasoning audit (same format as sprint audits)

148	
149	Refactor artifacts are committed directly — they don't go through the working dir →

---
name: util-artifact-schema
description: >
  Single source of truth for Chester's artifact naming, versioning, directory layout,
  and path resolution. Read this skill (don't invoke it) whenever you need to create,
  find, or reference a Chester artifact — design briefs, specs, plans, summaries, audits,
  or any other sprint artifact. If you're about to write a file path or filename for a
  Chester artifact, check here first.
---

# Artifact Schema

This document defines how Chester organizes work artifacts on disk. Every skill that
creates or reads artifacts follows these rules. When in doubt about where something
goes or what to call it, this is the authority.

## Config Resolution

Read project config before any artifact operations:

```bash
eval "$(chester-config-read)"
```

This exports two variables:

| Variable | Meaning | Example |
|----------|---------|---------|
| `CHESTER_WORKING_DIR` | Absolute path to gitignored scratch space — **all skills write here** | `/home/user/project/docs/chester/working` |
| `CHESTER_PLANS_DIR` | Relative path to archive target — **populated only at merge time** | `docs/chester/plans` |

**Directory model:** All artifact writes during a sprint go exclusively to `CHESTER_WORKING_DIR`.
No skill other than `finish-archive-artifacts` writes to `CHESTER_PLANS_DIR`.

- **Working directory** — the single write target for all pipeline work. Gitignored.
  Every skill in the design → spec → plan → execute → summary pipeline writes here.
  This directory lives outside worktrees so documents persist across branch switches
  and remain in the same location throughout the session.
- **Plans directory** — a merge-time archive, not an active destination. Tracked in git.
  `finish-archive-artifacts` copies the entire sprint subdirectory from working to plans
  exactly once, immediately before closing the worktree. No other skill touches this
  directory. Do not write artifact files here directly.

If `CHESTER_CONFIG_PATH` is `none`, no Chester config exists for this project. Run
`setup-start` first or use the defaults (`docs/chester/working/` and `docs/chester/plans/`).

## Sprint Naming

Every unit of work gets a sprint name that drives the directory name, branch name, and
file prefix.

**Format:** `YYYYMMDD-##-verb-noun-noun`

| Part | Rule | Example |
|------|------|---------|
| `YYYYMMDD` | Today's date | `20260407` |
| `##` | Next available sequence number for that date | `01` |
| `verb-noun-noun` | Three lowercase hyphenated words: a verb (the action) followed by two nouns (the target). Derived from the problem statement. | `strip-console-reports` |

To find the next sequence number:
```bash
ls "$CHESTER_WORKING_DIR/" 2>/dev/null | grep "^$(date +%Y%m%d)" | sort | tail -1
```

The sprint name is used for:
- **Directory name:** `{CHESTER_WORKING_DIR}/20260407-01-strip-console-reports/`
- **Branch name:** `20260407-01-strip-console-reports`
- **File prefix:** `strip-console-reports-{artifact}-{nn}.md`

Note that the file prefix uses only the three-word portion, not the date-sequence prefix.

## Directory Layout

Each sprint gets four subdirectories under the working directory:

```
{CHESTER_WORKING_DIR}/{sprint-subdir}/
├── design/     ← design briefs (proof envelope), thinking summaries, process evidence
├── spec/       ← specification documents, spec-stage ground-truth reports (opt-in)
├── plan/       ← implementation plans, threat reports, deferred items
└── summary/    ← session summaries, reasoning audits, cache analysis
```

Create the full structure when starting a new sprint:
```bash
mkdir -p "{CHESTER_WORKING_DIR}/{sprint-subdir}/design" \
         "{CHESTER_WORKING_DIR}/{sprint-subdir}/spec" \
         "{CHESTER_WORKING_DIR}/{sprint-subdir}/plan" \
         "{CHESTER_WORKING_DIR}/{sprint-subdir}/summary"
```

## File Naming

**Format:** `{sprint-name}-{artifact}-{nn}.md`

| Part | Rule |
|------|------|
| `{sprint-name}` | The three-word verb-noun-noun portion only (e.g., `strip-console-reports`) |
| `{artifact}` | The artifact type (see table below) |
| `{nn}` | Zero-padded version number: `00` is the original, `01`, `02`, `03` for revisions |

### Artifact Types

| Artifact | Directory | Purpose | Produced by |
|----------|-----------|---------|-------------|
| `design` | `design/` | Design brief — proof envelope (goal, necessary conditions, rules, permissions, evidence, industry context, risks, acceptance criteria). Templates live in each design skill: `design-large-task/references/design-brief-template.md` (8-section envelope) and `design-small-task/references/design-brief-small-template.md` (6-section lightweight). | `design-large-task` (8-section envelope), `design-small-task` (6-section lightweight) |
| `thinking` | `design/` | Thinking summary — decision history of how the proof reached its necessary conditions | `design-large-task` |
| `process` | `design/` | Process evidence — operational narrative (Understand Stage saturation history, Stage Transition timing, Solve Stage length) | `design-large-task` |
| `spec` | `spec/` | Specification document — architecture chosen from the brief's envelope, component structure, reuse profile, trade-off profile | `design-specify` |
| `spec-ground-truth-report` | `spec/` | Ground-truth findings — codebase verification of spec claims about existing code (opt-in review) | `design-specify` (ground-truth review stage) |
| `spec-skeleton` | `spec/` | Skeleton manifest — indexes test stubs by acceptance criterion ID (`AC-N.M`) for execute-write's structural trigger-check coverage diff. Filename: `{sprint-name}-spec-skeleton-{nn}.md`. | `design-specify` (scaffolding step); consumed by `execute-write` (trigger-check step) |
| `plan` | `plan/` | Implementation plan — task-by-task build instructions | `plan-build` |
| `plan-threat-report` | `plan/` | Combined plan-attack + plan-smell findings | `plan-build` (hardening phase) |
| `deferred` | `plan/` | Items deferred during execution | `execute-write` |
| `summary` | `summary/` | Session summary — what happened and why | `finish-write-records` |
| `audit` | `summary/` | Reasoning audit — decision-level trace | `finish-write-records` |

### State Files (Non-Artifact)

Some skills write JSON state files that are not versioned artifacts:

| File | Directory | Purpose |
|------|-----------|---------|
| `{sprint-name}-understanding-state.json` | `design/` | Understanding MCP state (Understand Stage nine-dimension saturation scoring) |
| `{sprint-name}-proof-state.json` | `design/` | Design Proof MCP state (Solve Stage necessary-conditions proof) |

## Versioning

- `00` is always the original
- When a skill revises an artifact (e.g., spec reviewer finds issues), it writes
  the next version: `01`, `02`, etc.
- The latest version is the current one — skills should read the highest-numbered
  version when loading an artifact
- Previous versions are kept for history, not deleted

## Refactor Artifacts

Refactoring work uses a separate directory and slightly different naming:

```
docs/refactor/{slug}/
├── {slug}-brief-00.md      ← evaluation brief (unique to refactors)
├── {slug}-summary-00.md    ← session summary (same format as sprint summaries)
└── {slug}-audit-00.md      ← reasoning audit (same format as sprint audits)
```

**Slug format:** `YYYYMMDD-##-word-word-word` (3-5 words, not fixed at four)

Refactor artifacts are committed directly — they don't go through the working dir →
plans dir archival flow because refactors don't use the sprint pipeline.

## Inheriting Sprint Context

When a skill is invoked mid-pipeline (not at the start), it inherits the sprint
subdirectory from the upstream artifact's path. For example, `plan-build` reads the
spec and derives the sprint subdir from the spec's parent directory. Skills should
not re-derive the sprint name when one is already established.

# Spec: Redesign Convergence Model — Lane 1 MVP

**Sprint:** 20260425-01-redesign-convergence-model
**Parent brief:** `docs/chester/working/20260425-01-redesign-convergence-model/design/redesign-convergence-model-design-00.md`
**Architecture:** Hybrid (principled merge from architect comparison)

## Goal

Add agent voice discipline (C1 Externalized Coverage; C2 Fact-default with marked Assumption/Opinion) to the shared `util-design-partner-role` skill so both `design-large-task` and `design-small-task` inherit the same discipline. Add three telemetry fields to the understanding MCP state for future Lane 2 retrospective analysis. Add a session metadata file written by `start-bootstrap` to link sprint artifacts to the JSONL transcript. Light-touch scope: five files modified, one test file created.

## Components

**Modified files:**
- `skills/util-design-partner-role/SKILL.md` — adds short composition introduction; new C1 section; new C2 section with before/after example; extends Self-Evaluation with four sibling checks
- `skills/start-bootstrap/SKILL.md` — adds Step 4c writing session metadata JSON
- `skills/design-large-task/understanding-mcp/state.js` — adds three telemetry array fields; updates `updateState` signature to accept warnings; adds three append operations
- `skills/design-large-task/understanding-mcp/server.js` — passes `validation.warnings` to `updateState` call
- `skills/design-large-task/SKILL.md` — adds one-line cross-reference to C1+C2 at Understand Stage Step 6 (Write commentary) and at Solve Stage Step 8 (Write commentary)
- `skills/design-small-task/SKILL.md` — adds one-line cross-reference to C1+C2 at Phase 4 Step 3 (Write commentary)

**New files:**
- `tests/test-understanding-telemetry.sh` — verifies three new telemetry fields persist across multiple submit cycles
- `tests/test-session-metadata.sh` — verifies session metadata file is written with all five required fields
- `tests/test-partner-role-discipline.sh` — verifies C1+C2 sections present in partner role with required elements

**Section ordering in `util-design-partner-role/SKILL.md` after edits:**
1. The Core Stance (unchanged)
2. The Interpreter Frame (unchanged)
3. **Composition Note** (new short paragraph — 2-3 sentences naming C1 and C2 as new disciplines and how they compose with existing rules)
4. Private Precision Slot (unchanged)
5. **C1: Externalized Coverage** (new section)
6. **C2: Fact Default with Marked Departures** (new section, includes before/after example)
7. Option-Naming Rule (unchanged)
8. Self-Evaluation (extended with four sibling checks)
9. Stance Principles (unchanged)

C1 and C2 placed between Private Precision Slot and Option-Naming Rule. Existing section ordering preserved (no reorganization beyond the insertion). Composition Note added near the top to signal the new disciplines exist and how they relate.

## Data Flow

**Per-turn agent flow with C1+C2 (after this sprint):**
1. Agent reads code privately (Research Boundary unchanged)
2. Agent extracts concept; if load-bearing for designer-facing conclusion, surfaces it in information package (C1)
3. Agent composes commentary; marks each claim per C2 default-vs-departure rule (Facts unmarked, Assumptions marked, Opinions marked; recommendations always carry Opinion marker)
4. Agent runs Translation Gate (unchanged) — strips code vocabulary from designer-visible output
5. Agent runs Self-Evaluation (extended) — answers strategy/code talk question + four C1/C2 sibling questions; fixes any yes-answers before sending

**Telemetry persistence flow per submit cycle:**
1. `handleSubmitUnderstanding` calls `validateUnderstandingSubmission` → returns `validation.warnings`
2. Calls `updateState(state, scores, validation.warnings)` (new third parameter)
3. `updateState` computes group saturation, overall saturation, transition status (existing logic)
4. After existing pushes to `scoreHistory` and `saturationHistory`, three new pushes: `groupSaturationHistory`, `transitionHistory` (object `{ ready, reasons }`), `warningsHistory` (warnings array passed in)
5. State persisted to disk via existing `saveState`
6. `handleGetState` returns full state including new fields via existing spread

**Session metadata write flow:**
1. `start-bootstrap` runs at sprint start
2. After Step 4b (breadcrumb write), Step 4c writes JSON file at `{CHESTER_WORKING_DIR}/{sprint-subdir}/design/{sprint-name}-session-meta.json`
3. JSON contains five fields: `sprintName`, `branchName`, `sessionStartTimestamp` (ISO 8601 UTC), `jsonlSessionId` (best-effort capture; null if unavailable), `skillVersion` (object with commit hashes for `util-design-partner-role` and `design-large-task` SKILL.md files)
4. File is tracked in git; `finish-archive-artifacts` copies it to `plans/` at sprint close

## Error Handling

- `updateState` `warnings` parameter defaults to `[]` for backward compatibility — any existing call site without the third argument continues to work
- Session metadata write does not fail bootstrap if `jsonlSessionId` cannot be captured (Claude Code env var unavailable) — write null or "unavailable" for that field, proceed
- Skill version capture (`git rev-parse`) runs with `git -C <repo-root>` to respect worktree-cd hazard; if git command fails, write null for that field rather than failing bootstrap
- `transitionHistory` entry normalizes `reasons` to empty array if `next.transition.reasons` is undefined (defensive against future changes to `checkTransitionReady`)
- Existing telemetry fields (`scoreHistory`, `saturationHistory`) and existing handler responses remain unchanged — additions are purely additive, no breaking changes to existing API surface

## Testing Strategy

Three new bash test files added to `tests/`:

- **`test-understanding-telemetry.sh`** — directly imports `state.js` functions via Node; calls `initializeState`, then three sequential `updateState` calls with valid scores; asserts array lengths grow correctly (1, 2, 3); asserts entry shapes are correct; asserts existing `scoreHistory` and `saturationHistory` still grow alongside. Covers AC-2.1 through AC-2.4.
- **`test-session-metadata.sh`** — invokes `start-bootstrap` simulation in temp directory; asserts metadata file exists at expected path; parses JSON; asserts all five required fields present. Covers AC-3.1 and AC-3.2.
- **`test-partner-role-discipline.sh`** — reads `util-design-partner-role/SKILL.md`; asserts presence of section headings (C1, C2); asserts key phrases (e.g., "load-bearing", "Fact default", "all recommendations are opinions"); asserts before/after example block exists; asserts Self-Evaluation section contains four sibling check questions. Covers AC-1.1 through AC-1.4.

**Test framework detection conflict noted:** Chester root has `package.json` (priority 2 in skeleton-generator → TypeScript), but actual test framework is bash (`tests/*.sh`, priority 4). Codebase convention and both architects' designs use bash. Skeletons emitted as bash to match implementation reality. Skeleton manifest documents this override.

## Constraints

- Translation Gate continues to apply to all designer-visible output unchanged; new C1 and C2 markers must use only plain-language vocabulary, no code names or file paths
- C2 markers use natural phrasing — agent has flexibility in how to express Assumption and Opinion; no canonical lead-words enforced beyond unambiguity
- Source breadcrumb in commentary explicitly forbidden ("Fact (read from BridgeService:45)" is REJECTED); precision lives in Private Precision Slot
- Citation system explicitly rejected — implementation must not introduce numbered inline markers, sources document, or derivation paragraphs even if they appear convenient
- Light-touch scope holds — only the five named files modified plus three test files created; no edits to gate logic, register restructuring, downstream skills, or any other Chester component
- Existing partner-role section text (Translation Gate, Research Boundary, Private Precision Slot, Option-Naming Rule) preserved verbatim — only positioning of new sections and Self-Evaluation extension affect existing content
- Three new telemetry fields are additive only; existing `scoreHistory`, `saturationHistory`, derived current-state fields, and all handler response shapes remain unchanged
- Session metadata file lives in tracked `design/` subdirectory — must NOT be added to gitignore
- Cross-reference in each per-turn flow limited to ONE LINE; large-task has two per-turn flows (Understand and Solve) so receives one line in each, totaling two lines

## Non-Goals

- Lane 2 work (transition gate redesign, dimension restructuring, Implication Closure prediction loop) — deferred to a separate follow-on sprint
- Citation system, numbered markers, sources document, derivation paragraphs — explicitly rejected during conversation
- Source breadcrumb in commentary — rejected
- Modifications to `design-specify`, `plan-build`, `execute-write`, or any downstream skill
- New MCP tools or schema changes beyond the three named telemetry fields
- Real-time topic-tracking, designer correction marking, per-turn topic-chosen capture
- Cross-MCP linkage between understanding state and proof state files
- Partner-role file reorganization beyond the targeted insertion of C1+C2 between Private Precision Slot and Option-Naming Rule, plus the short Composition Note near the top, plus Self-Evaluation extension

## Acceptance Criteria

### AC-1.1 — C1 section present in partner role
